payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Task-based reward structure definition
payload.justification: Signal SG916 describes the strategic process by which parents define what tasks earn what amounts.

payload.description: Task completion claiming
payload.justification: The core process of children claiming pocket money for completed tasks is stated in SG915.

payload.description: Earnings reconciliation with completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG919 describes the parental process of reconciling what was earned against what was completed for payment.

payload.description: Retrospective analysis of historical activity
payload.justification: Signal SG920 establishes the strategic process capability for looking back at past data for accountability and analysis.

payload.description: Child as pocket money claimant
payload.justification: Signal SG915 identifies children as entities who claim pocket money, a strategically important thing in the enterprise context.

payload.description: Task as unit of compensable work
payload.justification: Tasks are the fundamental entity that drives the monetary reward structure across signals SG915, SG916, and SG919.

payload.description: Monetary reward as task compensation
payload.justification: Monetary rewards are the strategically important compensation entity linking tasks to earnings.

payload.description: Task completion earns monetary reward
payload.justification: The causal relationship between task completion and earning pocket money is central to signals SG915 and SG919.

payload.description: Weekly period for task completion and earning accumulation
payload.justification: The week is the strategic cycle during which tasks are completed and earnings accumulate, referenced in both SG915 and SG918.

payload.description: Weekly period closure
payload.justification: Signal SG918 identifies period closure as the strategic event when children understand total weekly earnings.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are explicitly identified as strategic actors who claim money, view tasks, and see earnings across multiple signals.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are distinct strategic actors with capabilities to define rewards and reconcile payments in signals SG916 and SG919.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 declares this as the core strategic purpose of the enterprise.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into task opportunities and accomplishments
payload.justification: Signal SG917 articulates this motivational goal as a strategic driver.

payload.description: Enable children to understand their weekly earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG918 establishes transparency of earnings as a strategic goal at period end.

payload.description: Maintain historical accountability for task and payment activity
payload.justification: Signal SG920 defines the strategic need for historical accountability as a business goal.

payload.description: Payment based on verified task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG919 establishes the strategic principle that payment must be reconciled with actual completed tasks.

payload.description: Parent capability to define tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal describes business capability for parents to create and configure task entities.

payload.description: Child capability to view available tasks
payload.justification: Signal describes business requirement for children to access and view available task information.

payload.description: Child capability to view personally-completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal describes business requirement for children to access their completed task history.

payload.description: Aggregation and presentation of total earnings to child
payload.justification: Signal describes business function of calculating and displaying aggregated earnings.

payload.description: Parent reporting capability showing child-task-earnings data
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes parent-facing reporting as a business capability.

payload.description: Payment business process supported by parent reporting
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies payment as a business process requiring supporting data.

payload.description: Task-based pocket money claiming process throughout weekly cycle
payload.justification: Signal describes core business purpose as enabling a claiming process over time.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset at period boundaries
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes business rule governing task state reset behavior.

payload.description: Week-end aggregation and presentation of total earnings
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes business rule for earnings calculation and display timing.

payload.description: Retention requirement for historical task completion data
payload.justification: Signal describes business rule requiring preservation of historical data across time periods.

payload.description: Task as a business entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies task as an entity with value attributes in the business context.

payload.description: Child as a business entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies child as an entity participating in business relationships.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute of task entity
payload.justification: Signal describes monetary value as a defining attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Task availability state attribute
payload.justification: Signal references task availability state that is reset, indicating it is a managed attribute.

payload.description: Task completion status attribute
payload.justification: Signals reference completed versus available tasks and historical completion data, indicating completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Total earnings attribute for child entity
payload.justification: Signal describes aggregated earnings presented to child, indicating a calculated attribute.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for reporting and completion tracking
payload.justification: Signal explicitly establishes conceptual relationship between completed tasks and child entities. | Signal explicitly describes business relationship linking child, task, and earnings data.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle for pocket money claiming
payload.justification: Signal explicitly identifies weekly cycle as temporal boundary for pocket money process.

payload.description: Current week scope for task visibility
payload.justification: Signal specifies current week as temporal scope constraint for viewing tasks.

payload.description: Week-end event triggering earnings presentation
payload.justification: Signal identifies week-end as timing event for earnings aggregation and display.

payload.description: Weekly period boundary event triggering task reset
payload.justification: Signal identifies period boundary as timing event triggering availability reset.

payload.description: Parent as business actor
payload.justification: Signals explicitly identify parent as actor performing task definition and accessing reports.

payload.description: Child as business actor
payload.justification: Signals explicitly identify child as actor claiming pocket money, viewing tasks, and receiving earnings.

payload.description: Enable children to claim task-based pocket money
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines core business purpose and goal of the system.

payload.description: Task visibility limited to current week scope
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal constraint limiting which tasks are accessible within current period.

payload.description: Historical data must remain accessible across time periods
payload.justification: Signal describes business constraint requiring retention and accessibility of historical records.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Youth Economic Participation
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for establishing and governing the relationship through which children participate in household economic activity, defining their role as earners within a structured compensation framework.

payload.name: Compensable Work Definition
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to defining and maintaining what constitutes compensable work, the units by which effort is measured, and the reward structures that govern value exchange between participants.

payload.name: Performance Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to establish transparent mechanisms for claiming completion of work, reconciling accomplishments with earned compensation, and maintaining verifiable historical records of all performance and payment activity.

payload.name: Earnings Transparency
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to provide participants visibility into available opportunities, completed accomplishments, and resulting financial outcomes, enabling informed understanding of economic participation.

payload.name: Temporal Commitment Cycles
payload.description: Enterprise governance of periodic boundaries within which work occurs, earnings accumulate, and accountability periods close, establishing the temporal rhythm of economic participation.

payload.name: Task Specification Stewardship
payload.description: Responsibility for establishing and governing the definition, monetary valuation, and lifecycle boundaries of compensable work units within the household economy.

payload.name: Earnings Participation
payload.description: Responsibility for enabling child participation in the household task economy through visibility of opportunity, recording of completion, and accessibility of personal contribution history.

payload.name: Compensation Settlement
payload.description: Responsibility for determining, aggregating, and presenting earned compensation at period boundaries to enable settlement of obligations between household participants.

payload.name: Financial Oversight
payload.description: Responsibility for providing parental visibility into child participation, completion performance, and compensation outcomes to support payment decisions and household governance.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Responsibility for preserving the longitudinal integrity of task completion and earnings evidence across period boundaries to support ongoing accountability and retrospective inquiry.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish children as participants in household economic activity through pocket money claims.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural relationship between children and the household economic framework, establishing their role as claimants within the pocket money system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants in the pocket money framework.
payload.rationale: Establishes the parent role within the economic participation structure, necessary to define the complete set of enterprise-recognised actors.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to provide the capability for children to earn pocket money via task-based compensation, directly derived from the stated goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise tasks as the unit of compensable work.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental entity classification for compensable work at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish monetary rewards as compensation for tasks.
payload.rationale: Defines the nature and form of compensation within the enterprise value-exchange model.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall associate monetary rewards with task completion.
payload.rationale: Codifies the fundamental relationship between work completion and compensation entitlement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a task-based reward structure.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to ongoing governance of the compensation framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall base payment on verified task completion.
payload.rationale: Imposes a governing rule that payment is conditional upon verification, preventing unearned compensation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim completion of tasks.
payload.rationale: Process CCI in How column expressing the capability to claim task completion; obligation to enable the claim function at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall reconcile earnings with completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Process CCI in How column expressing the capability to reconcile two domain entities (earnings and completed tasks); fundamental accountability function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable retrospective analysis of historical task and payment activity.
payload.rationale: Process CCI in How column expressing the capability to perform retrospective analysis; obligation to enable review of past performance.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of task and payment activity.
payload.rationale: Why-column Goal CCI expressing accountability governance; a commitment to maintain records underpins the retrospective capability and establishes the enterprise's accountability posture.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children visibility into available task opportunities and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to enable transparency of task opportunities and accomplishments; derived from Why-column Goal expressing capability to provide visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to understand their weekly earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide understanding of financial outcomes from economic participation; derived from Why-column Goal expressing capability to enable earnings comprehension.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly period as the temporal boundary for task completion and earning accumulation.
payload.rationale: Defines the fundamental temporal structure (weekly period) that governs the enterprise's accountability cycle, establishing the entity and its role as the boundary for work and earnings.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall close each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's commitment to execute period closure as a definitive event that terminates the weekly accountability cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise Task as a compensable work unit.
payload.rationale: Establishes Task as a fundamental business entity with associated compensation obligation.

payload.statement: A Task shall have a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural attribute obligation surfaced from monetary value attribute CCI.

payload.statement: A Task shall have an availability state.
payload.rationale: Structural attribute obligation expressing the lifecycle state that governs claimability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce weekly task availability reset at period boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to govern task lifecycle according to temporal boundaries. How-column business process rule combined with When-column trigger event.

payload.statement: The business shall limit task visibility to the current week scope.
payload.rationale: Why-column governance constraint restricting temporal scope of task availability presentation.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing task opportunity visibility at the boundary, complementary to the actor-subject capability of viewing available tasks.

payload.statement: The system shall make personally-completed tasks visible to the respective child.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing personal contribution history visibility at the boundary, complementary to the actor-subject capability of viewing completed tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise a child as a participant in the task economy.
payload.rationale: Structural requirement establishing the child entity as a first-class business participant. Derived from What-column entity and Who-column actor CCIs defining the child's business role.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion status.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for maintaining the task completion attribute behind the boundary. What-column attribute obligation expressed as business record-keeping.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly cycle for pocket money claiming.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule defining the temporal constraint on claiming behaviour. When-column cycle expressed as business enforcement obligation.

payload.statement: The business shall scope task visibility to the current week.
payload.rationale: Business governance rule constraining task availability within the current weekly period. When-column temporal scope expressed as business constraint.

payload.statement: The business shall enable children to claim task-based pocket money.
payload.rationale: Business capability declaration fulfilling the goal of child participation in the task economy. Why-column goal expressed as business responsibility to enable the claiming capability.

payload.statement: The system shall make each child's total earnings visible to that child.
payload.rationale: Provides the black-box capability at the system boundary enabling children to view their aggregated earnings, supporting the goal of claimable task-based pocket money. The total earnings attribute (What) is made visible through the aggregation and presentation function (How) to fulfill the claiming goal (Why).

payload.statement: The business shall maintain the relationship between each child and their task earnings for completion tracking and reporting.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation defining the child-task-earnings relationship as a business artefact that must be maintained behind the boundary to support reporting and tracking responsibilities.

payload.statement: The business shall present aggregated total earnings at the end of each week.
payload.rationale: Temporal constraint governing when the business executes its earnings presentation responsibility. The week-end event (When) triggers the rule (How) requiring aggregation and presentation at that boundary.
payload.fit_criteria: Earnings aggregation and presentation occurs once per week at the week-end boundary.

payload.statement: The system shall make child-task-earnings data visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject statement expressing the black-box affordance that enables parental visibility. Complementary to the actor statement — names WHAT the system provides (visibility of the relationship), not HOW it is realised.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain the child-task-earnings relationship for reporting and completion tracking.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility to steward the What-column relationship artefact that supports reporting.

payload.statement: The business shall govern the payment process using parent reporting data.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the behind-boundary responsibility for the payment business process (How-column), supported by the reporting capability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly cycle for pocket money claiming.
payload.rationale: Business-subject statement expressing the temporal constraint (When-column) governing the claiming cycle — a business rule enforced behind the boundary.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion and earnings evidence across period boundaries.
payload.rationale: The weekly cycle process (How-007) and the retention requirement (How-010) together establish the business responsibility to preserve completion and earnings records beyond individual periods. This is a persistent business capability, not a workflow step.

payload.statement: The business shall make historical task completion and earnings data accessible for retrospective inquiry.
payload.rationale: Why-column CCI expressing a business responsibility to preserve accessibility across time. Stated as a business capability (making data accessible) rather than a prohibition, hence Functional rather than Constraint.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with monetary values.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs express a single unified obligation at the business boundary: the parent actor (Who-column) performing the capability (How-column) to define tasks with monetary values. This is an actor-subject statement (boundary test: the parent acts THROUGH the system, reaching in to define tasks). The verb 'can' expresses the actor's capability. The CCIs are from the same domain (D006) and describe complementary aspects of one business interaction.

payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Declares the core purpose of enabling children to claim pocket money for completed tasks throughout the week.
Articulates parental capability motivation to define task-based monetary reward structures.
Expresses the motivational goal of providing children visibility into their task opportunities and accomplishments.
States the purpose of enabling children to understand their weekly earnings at period closure.
Defines parental reporting need to reconcile earned amounts with completed tasks for payment administration.
Establishes business need for historical accountability and retrospective analysis capability.
Header indicating the following content describes the system at a conceptual business level.
Core business purpose defining the system as enabling children to claim task-based pocket money throughout a weekly cycle.
Business capability for parents to define tasks as entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Conceptual relationship establishing that completed tasks associate with child entities.
Business requirement for children to view both available and personally-completed task entities within the current week scope.
Week-end business rule requiring aggregation and presentation of total earnings to child stakeholders.
Parent-facing reporting capability showing child-task-earnings relationships to support payment business process.
Weekly cycle business rule resetting task availability state at period boundaries.
Business requirement for retention and accessibility of historical task completion data across time periods.
